text: Islamabad Day 1 OVER after 14+ hours. NO DEAL. Three rounds of direct talks (first US-Iran face-to-face since 1979). Hormuz control is the sticking point — Iran insists on sovereignty, US demands reopening. Some progress on Lebanon ceasefire conditions. Day 2 resumes Sunday. Source close to Tehran says US made 'unacceptable demands.'
verificationNote: CNBC, Washington Post, PBS confirmed talks concluded without deal. NPR, Al Jazeera corroborate.

text: Pope Leo XIV just held strongest anti-war statement yet — evening prayer vigil at St. Peter's denouncing 'delusion of omnipotence.' Clearly directed at Washington. Meanwhile Mojtaba Khamenei tells state TV 'our hands are on the trigger' and threatens 'new phase.' Both sides escalating rhetoric even as diplomats talk in Islamabad.
verificationNote: AP, Daily Caller confirmed Pope vigil. The News Pakistan, Al Jazeera confirmed Khamenei statement.

text: Hormuz mine clearance update: CENTCOM confirms 2 destroyers (USS Peterson DDG-121 + USS Murphy DDG-112) now inside the Gulf after transiting Hormuz. But the mine problem is staggering — Iran lost track of its OWN mines. 2,000-6,000 deployed, many drifted. Clearance: 6-10 weeks MINIMUM. Strait remains effectively closed for commercial traffic.
verificationNote: USNI News, Naval News, Axios confirmed transit. Japan Times, JPost confirmed mine drift issue.

text: Rubio arrested 3 Iranian nationals tied to 'Screaming Mary' (Ebtekar) from 1979 hostage crisis. EU sanctioned 19 Iranian officials. Netanyahu wants Hezbollah disarmament for Lebanon peace 'for generations.' And Pezeshkian says Iran will 'negotiate bravely.' A lot of diplomatic signals flying while bombs still fall on Lebanon.
verificationNote: State Dept, Fox News confirmed Rubio arrest. The National confirmed Kallas sanctions. Manila Times confirmed Netanyahu. ANI confirmed Pezeshkian.

text: CNN exclusive: US intelligence says China preparing to deliver MANPAD systems to Iran through third countries. If true, this is a massive escalation — shoulder-fired SAMs would threaten coalition low-altitude operations. China denied. This could torpedo Islamabad talks if confirmed publicly by the US delegation.
verificationNote: CNN exclusive confirmed. Bloomberg, Jerusalem Post corroborate. China denied via MFA spokesperson.

text: BREAKING: Islamabad talks COLLAPSE after 21 hours. Vance press conference at Serena Hotel: 'We have not reached an agreement. Bad news for Iran much more than for the US.' Left 'final and best offer' on the table. Nuclear weapons commitment + Hormuz control were the sticking points. Vance departed aboard Air Force Two at 07:08 local time.
verificationNote: NPR, AP, CNBC, Newsweek, Al Jazeera all confirmed talks collapse and Vance departure.

text: Iran Foreign Ministry response: 'The ball is in America's court.' Claims agreement on 'several points' but 'two important issues' remain. Ghalibaf calls talks 'unreasonable,' cites 'deep historical distrust.' Tasnim: Iran in no rush to return to the table. This framing is very different from Vance's — expect oil to spike Monday.
verificationNote: DimSumDaily, Times of Israel, Pakistan Today confirmed Iran response and Ghalibaf statement.

text: IRGC issues 'last warning' — 'strongly denies' any US warship transit of Hormuz. Vows 'strong response' to foreign military vessels. Meanwhile USS Murphy turned ON its AIS beacon (normally dark) — a deliberate transparency signal/challenge. This standoff is incredibly dangerous with the mine clearance underway.
verificationNote: Fortune, ANI, USNI News confirmed IRGC statement and AIS signal.

text: Alma Research Center: Hezbollah launched 105 attack waves on Israel April 11 — most intense day since war began. Drone struck apartment building in Shlomi. Meanwhile IDF 98th Division now in DIRECT CLASHES with Hezbollah mechanized forces at Bint Jbeil town center. This is urban warfare now.
verificationNote: JPost, L'Orient Today confirmed 105 attack waves and Bint Jbeil clashes.

text: Israel has approved an updated target list for energy/infrastructure strikes on Iran if diplomacy fails entirely. Iran counterthreats: reciprocal attacks that could 'plunge the region into darkness' — specifically naming Orot Rabin power plant (25% of Israel's electricity). Both sides escalating post-talks-collapse.
verificationNote: YNet, Haaretz confirmed Israel target list. Iran threat per Iranian media outlets.

text: Hormuz Day 41: Still ZERO oil tankers. Mine clearance continuing with USS Peterson + Murphy inside the Gulf. Navy deploying underwater drones to assist. But IRGC denying transit even happened and threatening 'strong response.' With Islamabad talks collapsed, the Hormuz situation just got much more dangerous. Goldman: $100+ Brent if shut another month.
verificationNote: Naval News, DefenseScoop, CENTCOM confirmed underwater drones and continued operations.

text: Netanyahu corruption trial resumes today — court REJECTED his delay request TWICE. Justice Friedman-Feldman: insufficient justification. Pezeshkian-Macron call: Iran entered with 'determination and seriousness,' outcome depends on US approach. France welcomes ceasefire, calls for end to Lebanon attacks. Diplomacy continues on every track despite Islamabad collapse.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Haaretz confirmed trial. Deccan Chronicle, Pravda France confirmed Pezeshkian-Macron call.

text: HISTORIC: 3 VLCCs (Serifos, Cospearl Lake, He Rong Hai) exit Hormuz — biggest oil movement since war began. ~6M barrels total. Cospearl Lake heading to Zhoushan, China with Iraqi oil. BUT 2 empty tankers (Agios Fanourios I, Shalamar) U-turned after talks collapsed. Mixed signals — ceasefire tanker optimism vs talks-collapse panic.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg confirmed VLCC transits and U-turns.

text: Israeli overnight strikes kill 24 across southern Lebanon. Teffahta: 13 dead. Qana: 5 dead. Maaroub: 6 dead. Dawn strikes on 5 more towns. Strikes INTENSIFYING after Islamabad talks collapse — The National explicitly links escalation to failed diplomacy. Lebanon death toll now 2,044+.
verificationNote: Xinhua, The National, TRT World confirmed all casualty figures.

text: Fars News: Iran has 'no plan for next round of negotiations' after Islamabad. Ghalibaf: blamed US for failure citing 'trust deficit.' Ceasefire expires ~April 22 with no diplomatic pathway. Meanwhile Rubio was at a UFC fight during the talks. This is heading toward expiration without renewal.
verificationNote: NPR, Al Jazeera, CNN confirmed talks collapse and Iran's 'no next round' stance.

text: Pope Leo XIV strongest denunciation yet: 'Enough of the idolatry of self and money. Enough of the display of power! Enough of war.' Called threats against Iranian people 'truly unacceptable.' Also: consumer sentiment 47.6 — lowest EVER recorded. Iran internet blackout Day 44 — longest nationwide shutdown in any country.
verificationNote: NPR, Vatican News, America Magazine confirmed Pope statements. CNBC, Axios confirmed consumer sentiment.

text: Market bracing for Monday: Goldman $100+ Brent if Hormuz shut another month. JPMorgan $150 if shut into mid-May. Two supertanker U-turns in Hormuz signal shipping panic returning. EIA April STEO: Q2 peak $115/bbl. S&P had its best week since November on ceasefire rally — all of that may unwind Monday after talks collapse.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, Goldman Sachs research note, JPMorgan warning confirmed by TheStreet.

text: Day 44 summary: Islamabad collapse aftermath dominates. Iran 'no next round.' 24 killed in Lebanon overnight. 3 VLCCs exit Hormuz but 2 U-turn. Trump: 'regardless we win.' Hegseth: 'ready to strike at moment's notice.' Ceasefire Day 5 but Lebanon front escalating. Oil $95.20/$95.50. Gas $4.166.
verificationNote: Compiled from NPR, Bloomberg, Xinhua, ABC News, Fortune.

text: CRITICAL: Trump announces BLOCKADE of Hormuz via Truth Social — 'effective immediately, US Navy will begin BLOCKADING any and all ships.' Orders interdiction of vessels that paid tolls to Iran. 'LOCKED AND LOADED.' This is a massive escalation from mine-clearing to full naval counter-blockade. Oil markets will go haywire Monday.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, CNBC, CBS News, Jerusalem Post all confirmed Trump blockade announcement.

text: Trump just declared a US BLOCKADE of Hormuz. This takes us from mine-clearing to full naval interdiction. IRGC response: 'Hormuz will not be opened.' Two navies now claiming blockade authority over the same strait. The 3 VLCCs that exited today may be the last ships through for weeks. Monday oil markets will be chaos.
verificationNote: Bloomberg blockade announcement confirmed. Al Jazeera liveblog confirmed Iran response.

text: Netanyahu video: campaign 'not over,' 'historic achievements.' Claims Israel killed 12 nuclear scientists. Meanwhile Russia gave Iran list of 55 Israeli energy targets per Ukrainian intel — including Orot Rabin (25% of Israel's power). Both sides preparing for renewed fighting as ceasefire frays.
verificationNote: Times of Israel confirmed Netanyahu. JPost, Militarnyi confirmed Russia-Iran intelligence sharing.

text: CSIS analysis: 'The Fragile US-Iran Ceasefire' — long-term costs to US likely felt outside Middle East. IISS warns conflict undermining entire nuclear non-proliferation regime. Meanwhile CPI 3.3%, consumer sentiment ALL-TIME LOW 47.6, gasoline +21.2% MoM (largest monthly spike since 1967). The economic blowback is accelerating.
verificationNote: CSIS published analysis confirmed. IISS Survival journal confirmed. BLS CPI data confirmed.

text: Day 44 EVENING: Trump BLOCKADE of Hormuz is the headline. Iran: 'will not be opened.' Netanyahu: 'not over.' Ghalibaf: 'we don't trust you.' Pope Leo: 'delusion of omnipotence.' Russia gave Iran 55 Israeli targets. Oil markets closed but Monday spike certain. Ceasefire expiring ~April 22 with no diplomatic pathway. Brent $95.20, WTI $95.50.
verificationNote: Compiled from Bloomberg, CNBC, Times of Israel, NPR, JPost, Al Jazeera, PBS.

text: IRGC just released a VIDEO showing vessels in crosshairs — warns any military vessels approaching Hormuz will be 'dealt with harshly and decisively.' Says enemy will be trapped in a 'deadly vortex.' This is the most explicit threat to US Navy operations since the war began. Trump blockade vs IRGC crosshairs video — Monday could be explosive.
verificationNote: Middle East Eye, Pakistan Today, Asharq Al-Awsat confirmed IRGC statement and video.

text: IDF confirms 200 airstrikes on Lebanon in past 24h — Bint Jbeil, Siddiqin, Nabatieh, Tyre district. Lebanese Health Ministry: 35 killed, 152 wounded, total now 2,055. Meanwhile Hezbollah launched 105 attack waves — RECORD DAY. 1 woman killed in northern Israel, 2 injured. The Lebanon front is completely out of control.
verificationNote: Middle East Monitor confirmed 2,055 toll. The National confirmed 200 strikes. LiveUAMap confirmed targets.

text: UK explicitly REJECTS joining US Hormuz blockade. Working with France on 40-nation freedom-of-navigation coalition instead. EU Kallas: 'no appetite — this is not Europe's war.' The transatlantic split on Hormuz strategy is widening. US may end up enforcing blockade alone — that changes the cost calculus dramatically.
verificationNote: Middle East Monitor, Pakistan Today confirmed UK rejection. The National confirmed Kallas statement.

text: Pezeshkian-Putin phone call after Islamabad collapse: Iran blames 'double standards and hegemonic approach.' Putin supports reparations demand, offers to mediate. Rubio on CBS clashed over nuclear intelligence. Hegseth: uranium handover 'non-negotiable.' Every actor escalating rhetoric. Ceasefire expires ~April 22 with zero pathway to renewal.
verificationNote: PressTV, Moscow Times confirmed Putin call. Fox News confirmed Rubio-CBS clash.

text: Day 44 NIGHT roundup: IRGC 'deadly vortex' crosshairs video. IDF 200 strikes on Lebanon (2,055 dead). Hezbollah 105 waves — record. UK rejects blockade. Pezeshkian-Putin call. Lebanon PM cancels US trip. Rubio-CBS nuclear clash. Gas $4.125. Brent $95.20. CPI 3.3%. Consumer sentiment ALL-TIME LOW. Ceasefire expiring in 10 days with no diplomatic track.
verificationNote: Compiled from Middle East Eye, Middle East Monitor, PressTV, Moscow Times, Fox News, AAA, Trading Economics.
